zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Herbert Finch於1405年出生在英國肯特時，她的父親Vincent Finch IV39歲，母親Isabella de Cralle39歲。她於1422年與Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt在英國肯特斯可也斯 霍爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1457年11月20日在英國肯特吉林加姆享年52歲，埋葬在英國肯特布拉布恩。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Herbert Finch于1405年出生在英国肯特时，她的父亲Vincent Finch IV39岁，母亲Isabella de Cralle39岁。她于1422年与Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt在英国肯特斯可也斯 霍爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1457年11月20日在英国肯特吉林加姆享年52岁，埋葬在英国肯特布拉布恩。

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, and French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In Britain, this Old French name, introduced by the Normans, reinforced the less common Old English cognate Herebeorht. The surname was taken to Ireland after the Anglo-Norman invasion and in the 16th century.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Harbison
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name Herbert (the change from -er- to -ar- was a common one in Old French and Middle English). An intrusive n later gave Harbinson .

variants.name: Hebard
meanings: English (Surrey):
variant of Herbert , with assimilation of the first r. Compare Hebb .
variant of   Hibbert  .

variants.name: Harbert
meanings: German and English: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbers
meanings: German and Dutch: patronymic from   Herbert  . It is found mainly in Lower Saxony and across the Dutch border.

variants.name: Dickherber
meanings: Probably a nickname for a fat person by the name of Herbert , of which Herber is a short form, or, more likely, in the north from Die(c)kherber, a topographic and distinguishing name for a Herber living by the dike, see Dicke 2. Compare Dickmeyer .

variants.name: Hebert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Lucien, Michel, Gaston, Alcide, Camille.
French (Hébert): assimilated form of Herbert . Compare Abair , Abar , Abare , Abear , Aber , Eber , and Ebert .
German: variant of   Heber   1.

variants.name: Herber
meanings: German and Dutch: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbertson
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name   Herbert  .

variants.name: Hebb
meanings: English (East Yorkshire and northeast Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Hebb, Ebb, pet forms of Herbert .

variants.name: Herb
meanings: South German: nickname for a harsh or rough person, from Middle High German herb ‘tart, harsh’.
German: from the personal name Herpo, a pet form of Herbert .

nameMeanings.name: Finch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English finch, fink ‘finch’ (Old English finc), for a small, lively, cheerful person.

Americanized form (translation into English) of German, Jewish (Ashkenazic), Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Fink ‘finch’, or of some of the corresponding Slavic surnames, e.g. Czech (Moravian) Pěnka (see Penka ).

variants.name: Fink
meanings: German, Jewish (Ashkenazic), Danish, and English: nickname for a lively and cheerful person, or, in the case of the Jewish name, an artificial name, from Middle High German vinke, Middle English and Yiddish fink ‘finch’ (see Finch and Vink ). The surname of German origin is also found in some central European countries, for example in Czechia, Slovenia, and Croatia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic nicknames or surnames. In part, this is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Herbert Finch was born in 1405, in Kent, England, her father, Vincent Finch IV, was 39 and her mother, Isabella de Cralle, was 39. She married Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt in 1422, in Scot's Hall, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 20 November 1457, in Gillingham, Kent, England, at the age of 52, and was buried in Brabourne, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Herbert Finch est née en 1405, à Kent, Angleterre, son père, Vincent Finch IV, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Isabella de Cralle, avait 39 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt en 1422, à Scot's Hall, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 novembre 1457, à Gillingham, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Brabourne, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Herbert Finch 1405 in Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Vincent Finch IV 39 und ihre Mutter Isabella de Cralle 39. Sie hat 1422 in Scot's Hall, Kent, England Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 20. November 1457 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Gillingham, Kent, England und wurde in Brabourne, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Herbert Finch è nata il nel 1405 nel Kent, England, sua padre, Vincent Finch IV, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Isabella de Cralle, aveva 39 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt nel 1422, nel Scot's Hall, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 20 novembre 1457, nel Gillingham, Kent, England, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolta a Brabourne, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Herbert Finchが1405年にイギリスケントで生まれた時，父Vincent Finch IVは39歳，母Isabella de Cralleは39歳であった。1422年にイギリスケントスコト'ス ハルでSir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1457年11月20日にイギリスケントギリンガムで52歳で死去し，イギリスケントブラボウルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Herbert Finch(이)가 1405년 영국 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Vincent Finch IV(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella de Cralle(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 1422년 영국 켄트 스컷'스 홀에서 Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1457년 11월 20일 영국 켄트 길링함에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 브라보우른에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Herbert Finch nasceu em 1405, em Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Vincent Finch IV, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Isabella de Cralle, tinha 39 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt em 1422, em Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de novembro de 1457, em Gillingham, Kent, Inglaterra, com 52 anos, e foi sepultada em Brabourne, Kent, Inglaterra.

ro.Scots Hall, Brabourne, Kent, England: Scot's Hall, Kent, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Herbert Finch родилась в 1405 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Vincent Finch IV было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Cralle, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt в 1422 г. в местности скот'с холл, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 20 ноября 1457 г. в местности джиллингхам, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности брабурн, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Herbert Finch nació en 1405, en Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Vincent Finch IV, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Isabella de Cralle, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt en 1422, en Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 20 de noviembre de 1457, en Gillingham, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultada en Brabourne, Kent, Inglaterra.
